scene_description:
**Foreground (closest to camera):**  
- **Road surface:** Paved asphalt with visible cracks in the center, two solid yellow center lines, and white edge lines on both sides.  
- **Right side:** A yellow fire hydrant on grass (cylindrical, with a cap on top). Adjacent to the hydrant, a dark gray pickup truck is parked on a driveway (front wheel and side visible; no visible license plate). A wooden fence (vertical planks, weathered brown) runs parallel to the road behind the truck.  
- **Left side:** A light gray concrete sidewalk (cracked) runs parallel to the road. Between the sidewalk and road, a narrow strip of well-maintained green grass. To the left of the sidewalk, dense green shrubs and a portion of a tree with green leaves (left edge of the image).  

**Midground (middle of the scene):**  
- **Left side:** Two houses. The closer house is single-story with gray shingle siding, white window trim, a gray shingled roof, and a brick chimney. It is partially obscured by dense green shrubs. Further back, a larger house with similar gray shingle siding, multiple windows (some on the second story), and a gray shingled roof. A concrete sidewalk continues along the left edge with a grassy strip between the sidewalk and road.  
- **Right side:** A two-story house with light gray shingle siding, white window trim (some windows have black shutters), a gray shingled roof, and a white front porch with railings. A white SUV is parked in the driveway. A wooden fence (vertical planks, weathered brown) runs behind the house. A covered structure with a dark gray tarp is visible behind the fence.  
- **Road:** A dark-colored vehicle (e.g., sedan) drives away in the center of the road (distant midground).  
- **Infrastructure:** Wooden utility poles (with a single horizontal crossbar) line the left side of the road, supporting multiple black wires (4–5 visible).  

**Background (distant end of the road):**  
- **Road:** The two-lane road extends into the distance with yellow center lines and white edge lines.  
- **Buildings:** Additional houses (smaller, with gray or white shingle roofs) are visible further down the road.  
- **Vegetation:** Dense green deciduous trees line both sides of the road, forming a canopy. Tall trees are visible behind the houses on both sides.  
- **Sky:** Light blue with scattered white clouds.  

**Text & Signs:** No visible text on signs, poles, or vehicles. No road name signs, shop signs, banners, or stickers.  

**Infrastructure (additional):**  
- No bollards, street lamps, traffic lights, or guardrails (only the wooden fence on the right).  

**Vehicles & Plates:**  
- Dark gray pickup truck (right foreground, no visible license plate).  
- White SUV (right midground, parked, no visible license plate).  
- Dark-colored vehicle driving away (center midground, no visible license plate).  

**Road Surface & Markings:**  
- Two solid yellow center lines (no passing).  
- White edge lines on both sides (separating road from grassy shoulders).  
- Asphalt road surface with cracks; no visible curbs.  
- Right-hand traffic (evidenced by the layout of the road, sidewalk, and absence of left-side traffic features).  

**Buildings & Architecture:**  
- All houses have pitched (gabled) roofs; shingle siding in gray or white; rectangular windows (some with black shutters).  
- Right-side two-story house has a white front porch with railings.  

**Vegetation & Terrain:**  
- Dense green deciduous trees and well-maintained grass lawns on both sides.  
- Flat terrain (road is level with no visible elevation changes).

details.name: yellow center lines
details.reason: Two solid yellow lines are a specific road marking characteristic of North American roads.
details.focused_description: Two parallel yellow lines run along the center of the road. The lines are painted on asphalt, appear slightly faded in sections with visible cracking around them, and are continuous (with the lower portion of the image showing motion blur). The lines are straight in the visible non-blurred section. No text is present on the lines.

details.name: white edge lines
details.reason: The combination of yellow center lines and white edge lines is standard in North American road infrastructure.
details.focused_description: Two white edge lines are visible: one on the left side of the road (separating the road from the sidewalk and grass) and one on the right side (separating the road from the grassy area). Both are straight, continuous, and painted on asphalt. Color: white. Material: paint. Condition: slightly worn but clearly visible, with some minor fading in sections. No text is present on the lines.

details.name: wooden utility pole
details.reason: Wooden utility poles with horizontal crossbars are typical for North American utility networks.
details.focused_description: A tall, cylindrical wooden utility pole with a weathered brown color. The pole is vertical, made of wood, and shows signs of aging. It features horizontal metal crossarms near the top supporting power lines. No visible text is present on the pole.

details.name: yellow fire hydrant
details.reason: The presence of a yellow fire hydrant can be a clue for specific regional utility standards.
details.focused_description: A yellow fire hydrant with a bright yellow body, rounded conical top, and two side nozzles. One nozzle features a blue band around it. The hydrant appears to be made of metal, in good condition, with no visible signs of damage or wear. The main body is cylindrical, and the top is a tapered dome shape.

details.name: shingle siding
details.reason: Shingle siding on residential houses is a common architectural element in the US and Canada.
details.focused_description: Light gray horizontal shingle siding with weathered appearance, rectangular panels, no visible text, made of wood (or similar material), showing signs of aging and exposure.
